text:
Once upon a time in the small village of Willowbrook, there was an old tree that had observed every marriage end and every betrayal. It was a wise tree, with gnarled branches that held secrets.

There was a young farmer named Tom, who often visited the tree to hear its wisdom. One day, Tom returned wearing sunglasses, a hat, and a confident grin, carrying a mysterious package.

"Why such shades, Tom?" the tree asked.

Tom didn't answer. He simply said, "I've got a surprise for you." He opened it up to reveal a small, sparkling crystal.

"That's no ordinary crystal," the tree remarked. "It's a healing gem. Use it to talk things over with your wife."

Tom looked at it curiously. "You think she'd listen?"

The tree chuckled. "Try it in your pocket. Walk her down to that pond. Just say, 'Little crystal gave me ideas.'"
